message:
test(e2e): add hooks, safety uninstall, and webhook E2E tests

Adds three new E2E test suites covering hooks lifecycle, safety
uninstall, and webhook delivery with retry and HMAC signature
verification.

- e2e/hooks_test.go (270 lines): E2E tests for ntm hooks install,
  status, and uninstall commands verifying JSON output, hook file
  creation, and idempotent operations
- e2e/safety_uninstall_test.go (131 lines): E2E tests for ntm safety
  uninstall command verifying removal of safety hooks, JSON response
  structure, and cleanup behavior
- e2e/webhook_test.go (383 lines): E2E tests for webhook delivery
  including HMAC-SHA256 signature verification, retry on failure,
  delivery timeout handling, and httptest-based server fixtures
- .beads/issues.jsonl: update bd-1bxza (Ensemble UX E2E) to in_progress

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

### E2E Test Scenarios

   - Verify agent continues working

3. **Multi-agent context tracking**
   - Spawn 3 agents
   - Each at different context levels
   - Verify per-agent alerts

4. **ntm context command**
   - Run ntm context myproject
   - Verify accurate display
   - Test --json flag

 {"id":"bd-1bruk","title":"PromptInjector: Support review-mode prompts for gmi agents","description":"## Purpose
Generate review-mode prompts that instruct agents to analyze and provide feedback on existing code rather than making changes, supporting quality review workflows.

## Implementation Details

```go
// ReviewPromptGenerator creates review-mode prompts for agents
type ReviewPromptGenerator struct {
    templates map[string]string // agent_type -> template
    logger    *slog.Logger
}

// Default review templates per agent type
var defaultReviewTemplates = map[string]string{
    "cc": `Review the code in this project. Focus on:
1. Potential bugs or edge cases
2. Security vulnerabilities
3. Performance concerns
4. Code clarity and maintainability

Do NOT make any changes. Only provide analysis and recommendations.`,
    
    "cod": `Analyze the codebase and identify:
- Code quality issues
- Missing error handling
- Test coverage gaps
- Documentation needs

Output findings only, no modifications.`,
    
    "gmi": `Perform a code review focusing on:
- Architecture patterns
- API design
- Dependency concerns
- Scalability considerations

Analysis mode only - no code changes.`,
}

// GenerateReviewPrompt creates a review prompt for the given context
func (g *ReviewPromptGenerator) GenerateReviewPrompt(agentType, projectPath string, opts ReviewOptions) string {
    template := g.templates[agentType]
    if template == "" {
        template = defaultReviewTemplates[agentType]
    }
    
    prompt := template
    if opts.FocusArea != "" {
        prompt = fmt.Sprintf("Focus specifically on: %s\n\n%s", opts.FocusArea, prompt)
    }
    if opts.FilePattern != "" {
        prompt = fmt.Sprintf("Review files matching: %s\n\n%s", opts.FilePattern, prompt)
    }
    
    g.logger.Info("generated review prompt",
        "agent_type", agentType,
        "project", filepath.Base(projectPath),
        "focus_area", opts.FocusArea,
        "file_pattern", opts.FilePattern,
        "prompt_len", len(prompt))
    
    return prompt
}

type ReviewOptions struct {
    FocusArea   string // e.g., "security", "performance"
    FilePattern string // e.g., "*.go", "internal/**"
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log prompt generation with agent type and project
- Log any customization options applied
- Log final prompt length (not content)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Distinct review templates per agent type
- [ ] Templates explicitly forbid code changes
- [ ] Support for focus area customization
- [ ] Support for file pattern scoping
- [ ] Custom template override capability
- [ ] Unit tests for each agent type template","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:00:44.480042400Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T03:35:53.566912750Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T03:35:53.566865331Z","close_reason":"Complete: ReviewPromptGenerator implemented with all acceptance criteria met - distinct templates per agent type, no-changes enforcement, focus area/file pattern options, custom override capability, and comprehensive unit tests for all agent types.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1bruk","depends_on_id":"bd-28cpd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1bruk","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1bsp3","title":"Feature: ntm scrub (scan NTM artifacts for leaked secrets)","description":"## Summary
Add a command to scan *existing* NTM artifacts (prompt history, event logs, archives, support bundles) for secret patterns and report findings.

## Why
Even after adding redaction, there may already be historical artifacts containing sensitive strings. Users need a way to assess exposure.

## Behavior
- Read-only by default: report findings with file + offset + category.
- Optional `--redact-in-place` mode (future) should be explicit and produce backups.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can scan bounded time windows/paths.
- Produces machine-readable output for automation.
- Unit tests with synthetic fixtures.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:30:54.949390144Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:30:54.972936635Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1bsp3","depends_on_id":"bd-27hn0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:37Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1bz6d","title":"BeadScanner: Execute br list --status open --json per project","description":"## BeadScanner: Execute br list --status open --json per project

### Purpose
Implement the actual bead counting via br CLI subprocess calls.

### Implementation Details

**File:** Part of `internal/swarm/bead_scanner.go`

**br CLI Integration:**
```go
// countBeads runs br list and parses the JSON output.
func (s *BeadScanner) countBeads(ctx context.Context, projectPath string) (int, error) {
    // Build command: br list --status open --json
    cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, s.brPath, "list", "--status", "open", "--json")
    cmd.Dir = projectPath
    
    // Capture output
    output, err := cmd.Output()
    if err != nil {
        // br might not be installed or project has no .beads
        if exitErr, ok := err.(*exec.ExitError); ok {
            // Non-zero exit is OK - might just mean no beads
            log.Debug("[BeadScanner] br_exit_error", 
                "project", projectPath, 
                "code", exitErr.ExitCode())
        }
        return 0, nil // Treat as 0 beads, not an error
    }
    
    // Parse JSON array
    var beads []json.RawMessage
    if err := json.Unmarshal(output, &beads); err != nil {
        // Might be empty or malformed
        log.Warn("[BeadScanner] json_parse_error", 
            "project", projectPath, 
            "error", err)
        return 0, nil
    }
    
    return len(beads), nil
}
```

**Expected br Output:**
```json
[
  {"id": "bd-xxx", "title": "...", "status": "open", ...},
  {"id": "bd-yyy", "title": "...", "status": "open", ...}
]
```

**Parallel Scanning:**
```go
// scanAll scans all projects concurrently.
func (s *BeadScanner) scanAll(ctx context.Context, projects []string) []ProjectBeadCount {
    results := make([]ProjectBeadCount, len(projects))
    var wg sync.WaitGroup
    sem := make(chan struct{}, s.Parallelism) // Limit concurrency
    
    for i, project := range projects {
        wg.Add(1)
        go func(idx int, proj string) {
            defer wg.Done()
            sem <- struct{}{}        // Acquire
            defer func() { <-sem }() // Release
            
            count, err := s.countBeads(ctx, proj)
            if err != nil {
                log.Warn("[BeadScanner] scan_error", "project", proj, "error", err)
            }
            
            results[idx] = ProjectBeadCountFromPath(proj, count)
            log.Info("[BeadScanner] project_scanned", "project", proj, "beads", count)
        }(i, project)
    }
    
    wg.Wait()
    return results
}
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log br call: `[BeadScanner] br_call project=%s`
- Log br result: `[BeadScanner] br_result project=%s beads=%d`
- Log br error: `[BeadScanner] br_error project=%s error=%v`
- Log parse error: `[BeadScanner] json_parse_error project=%s`

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Execute br list --status open --json
- [ ] Parse JSON array and count elements
- [ ] Handle br not installed gracefully
- [ ] Handle projects without .beads gracefully
- [ ] Concurrent scanning with configurable parallelism","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T06:59:34.093978854Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T02:29:56.644658245Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:29:56.640839271Z","close_reason":"Implemented in bead_scanner.go - countBeads function uses br list --status open --json with concurrent scanning, JSON parsing, and graceful error handling","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1bz6d","depends_on_id":"bd-2xinm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1bz6d","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1c6d7","title":"DCG: Implement send command interception for non-Claude agents","description":"Intercept 'ntm send' commands and run through DCG before forwarding to pane.

## Why Needed

Codex and Gemini agents don't have PreToolUse hooks like Claude Code.
We need to intercept at the NTM level when sending commands to their panes.

## Implementation

### In ntm send command:
1. Before sending to pane, check if target is non-Claude agent
2. If DCG enabled, extract command portion from send payload
3. Run: dcg check --command "<extracted_command>"
4. If dcg returns non-zero, block send and return error
5. If dcg returns zero, proceed with normal send

### Challenges
- Identifying "command" content in arbitrary send payloads
- Some sends are just text, not commands
- Need heuristic: if starts with common command patterns, check DCG

### Heuristics for command detection
- Starts with: git, rm, mv, cp, chmod, chown, kubectl, terraform
- Contains: sudo, -rf, --force, --hard
- Looks like shell: contains |, &&, ||, ;

### Code Location
- internal/commands/send.go (add DCG check)
- internal/integrations/dcg/check.go (dcg invocation wrapper)

## Testing
- Send safe command to Codex pane, verify passes
- Send dangerous command to Codex pane, verify blocked
- Send arbitrary text (not command), verify passes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:45:10.955702295Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:44:21.649341702Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:44:21.649289824Z","close_reason":"Implemented DCG interception in send.go with command extraction heuristics and audit logging","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1c6d7","depends_on_id":"bd-1m6sw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1c6d7","depends_on_id":"bd-2q9px","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1c6d7","depends_on_id":"bd-o97j9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1c9ib","title":"RCH Integration: Remote Compilation Helper for Build Offloading","description":"Integrate RCH (remote_compilation_helper) to offload compilation to remote workers.

## Project Location
/dp/remote_compilation_helper (Rust CLI)

## Problem Being Solved

Multi-agent workflows often trigger parallel builds:
- Agent 1: cargo build on project A
- Agent 2: go build on project B
- Agent 3: cargo test on project A

This causes:
- CPU saturation (all cores maxed)
- Thermal throttling
- Slow agent response times
- Failed builds due to resource contention

## What RCH Does

RCH transparently offloads compilation to remote workers:

### Architecture
1. PreToolUse hook intercepts cargo/go build commands
2. RCH client checks for available workers
3. If worker available, rsync project and run remotely
4. Stream output back to local terminal
5. Rsync artifacts back when done

### Key Features
- Transparent: no agent changes needed
- Deduplication: same build on same worker reuses cache
- Fallback: if no workers, run locally
- Worker fleet: multiple machines can serve as workers

### Key RCH Commands
```bash
rch status            # Show worker status
rch workers list      # List available workers
rch build cargo       # Explicit remote build
rch config            # Configure workers
```

## Integration Strategy for NTM

### 1. PreToolUse Hook (Primary)
Configure hook for all agents to route builds through RCH.

### 2. Dashboard Widget
Show compilation status: local vs remote builds, worker utilization, time saved.

### 3. Robot Mode Commands
- --robot-rch-status: Get RCH and worker status
- --robot-rch-workers: List workers

## Considerations
- Network latency: remote only wins for long builds (>10s)
- Security: workers must be trusted (code execution)
- Artifacts: some builds produce local-only files
- DCG: RCH commands should be whitelisted

## Acceptance Criteria

1. RCH integration module exists at internal/integrations/rch/
2. PreToolUse hook configuration for cargo/go build interception works
3. Dashboard widget shows local vs remote build stats
4. Robot mode commands return valid JSON with worker status
5. Fallback to local build works when no workers available
6. All child beads (bd-1j956, bd-64v8u, bd-1o1q1, bd-398bh, bd-3u84u, bd-1ghcp) completed

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/integrations/rch/rch_test.go
- Test worker detection, build routing, fallback logic

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
# [E2E-RCH] RCH Integration Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-RCH]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing RCH integration"
# Test robot-rch-status command
ntm --robot-rch-status | jq -e '.rch_installed \!= null' || exit 1
echo "$LOG_PREFIX RCH status check passed"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. rch binary detection and version check
2. Worker availability monitoring
3. Build offloading transparent to agents
4. Session stats tracked
5. Config section [integrations.rch] works
6. Graceful fallback when rch missing
7. Unit tests for detection and offloading
8. E2E test for full RCH integration","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:06:45.277322518Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:13:01.921527712Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1c9ib","depends_on_id":"bd-1k3ga","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-r6d5e","title":"E2E Tests: ntm spawn --assign full workflow","description":"## Test Coverage
Full end-to-end test of ntm spawn --assign convenience flag.

## Test Scenario
1. Create test workspace with beads
2. Run ntm spawn --assign (single command)
3. Verify agents spawned
4. Verify beads assigned
5. Verify prompts received by agents
6. Verify combined output

## Test Script (tests/e2e/test_spawn_assign.sh)
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

LOG=/tmp/ntm-e2e-spawn-assign-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log
exec > >(tee -a $LOG) 2>&1

echo "[INFO] Starting ntm spawn --assign E2E test at $(date)"

# Setup
echo "[STEP 1] Creating test workspace..."
TEST_DIR=/tmp/ntm-spawn-assign-test-$$
mkdir -p $TEST_DIR
cd $TEST_DIR
br init
for i in {1..4}; do br create "Test bead $i" --type task; done
echo "[OK] Created 4 test beads"

# Spawn + Assign in one command
echo "[STEP 2] Running ntm spawn --assign..."
RESULT=$(ntm spawn ntm-spawn-assign-$$ --cc=2 --no-user --assign --strategy=round-robin --json)
echo "[DEBUG] Result: $RESULT"

# Verify spawn
echo "[STEP 3] Verifying spawn..."
AGENT_COUNT=$(echo $RESULT | jq '.spawn.agents | length')
if [ "$AGENT_COUNT" -ne 2 ]; then
    echo "[FAIL] Expected 2 agents, got $AGENT_COUNT"
    exit 1
fi
echo "[OK] 2 agents spawned"

# Verify assign
echo "[STEP 4] Verifying assignments..."
ASSIGNED=$(echo $RESULT | jq '.assign.assigned | length')
if [ "$ASSIGNED" -lt 2 ]; then
    echo "[FAIL] Expected at least 2 assignments, got $ASSIGNED"
    exit 1
fi
echo "[OK] $ASSIGNED beads assigned"

# Cleanup
echo "[STEP 5] Cleaning up..."
ntm kill ntm-spawn-assign-$$
rm -rf $TEST_DIR
echo "[OK] Cleanup complete"

echo "[PASS] ntm spawn --assign E2E test passed at $(date)"
```

## Logging Requirements
- Timestamp each step
- Log combined output
- Log spawn and assign phases separately
- Save full log to /tmp

 {"id":"bd-r9fy","title":"Implement global animation tick source for synchronized animations","description":"## Problem
Multiple animated components with independent tick counters can cause visual noise.

## Code Review Findings (2026-01-19)

### Good News: Dashboard Already Uses Global Tick

**Central tick source exists:**
- `dashboard.go:42-43` - `DashboardTickMsg` message type  
- `dashboard.go:224` - `animTick int` in Model
- `dashboard.go:745` - Single 100ms tick: `tea.Tick(time.Millisecond*100, ...)`
- `dashboard.go:1599-1600` - Handler increments `m.animTick++`

**All animations use the global tick:**
```go
// dashboard.go:2618 - progress bar uses m.animTick
barContent := styles.ShimmerProgressBar(percent/100.0, barWidth, "█", "░", m.animTick, colors...)

// dashboard.go:2142 - ticker panel receives animTick
m.tickerPanel.SetAnimTick(m.animTick)

// layout.go:238 - layout functions receive tick parameter
bar := styles.ShimmerProgressBar(percent/100, width-4, "█", "░", tick, ...)

// dashboard.go:2718 - spinner uses animTick
statusIcon = WorkingSpinnerFrame(m.animTick)
```

### Orphaned Code in components/progress.go

The `components/progress.go` file has UNUSED independent tick mechanisms:
- Line 132: 50ms tick for ProgressBar
- Line 166: 50ms tick for IndeterminateBar
- Line 224: 50ms tick for IndeterminateBar.Init()

**These are NOT used by the dashboard.** The dashboard uses `styles.ShimmerProgressBar()` directly, which takes a tick parameter.

## Updated Fix Approach

### 1. Clean Up Orphaned Code
```go
// components/progress.go - Consider deprecating or removing the independent tick
// since dashboard uses styles.ShimmerProgressBar() with global tick instead

// Option A: Deprecate
// Deprecated: Use styles.ShimmerProgressBar() with global tick instead
func (p ProgressBar) Init() tea.Cmd { ... }

// Option B: Remove entirely if unused
```

### 2. Document Global Tick Pattern
Add to CONTRIBUTING.md:
```markdown
## Animation Synchronization

All animations in the dashboard use a global tick source:

1. The dashboard maintains `animTick int` incremented every 100ms
2. Pass `animTick` to all animated rendering functions
3. Never create independent tea.Tick sources in subcomponents
4. Use `styles.Shimmer()`, `styles.ShimmerProgressBar()` with tick parameter

Example:
\`\`\`go
// Good: use global tick
bar := styles.ShimmerProgressBar(percent, width, "█", "░", m.animTick, colors...)

// Bad: independent tick in subcomponent
func (m MyModel) Init() tea.Cmd {
    return tea.Tick(time.Millisecond*50, ...) // Creates separate timer
}
\`\`\`
```

### 3. Add Test for Tick Synchronization
```go
func TestAllAnimationsUseGlobalTick(t *testing.T) {
    // Verify that styles.Shimmer produces deterministic output for given tick
    result1 := styles.Shimmer("test", 42, "#FF0000", "#00FF00")
    result2 := styles.Shimmer("test", 42, "#FF0000", "#00FF00")
    
    if result1 != result2 {
        t.Error("Shimmer should be deterministic for same tick value")
    }
    
    // Different ticks produce different results
    result3 := styles.Shimmer("test", 43, "#FF0000", "#00FF00")
    if result1 == result3 {
        t.Error("Different ticks should produce different shimmer effects")
    }
}
```

## Verification Checklist
- [x] Dashboard uses single DashboardTickMsg source (100ms)
- [x] animTick passed to all animated components  
- [ ] Remove/deprecate orphaned tick code in components/progress.go
- [ ] Document global tick pattern in CONTRIBUTING.md
- [ ] Verify NTM_REDUCE_MOTION=1 disables all animation

## Priority Adjustment
Changed to P3 - cleanup/documentation task. Added deterministic test for same tick value. components/progress.go is a valid standalone component, not orphaned - can be used outside dashboard context.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-r9fy","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-rcwdt","title":"Task: Implement --robot-ensemble-synthesize for triggering synthesis","description":"# Task: Implement --robot-ensemble-synthesize for triggering synthesis

Trigger Stage 3 synthesis for an ensemble session via robot mode.

## Command Specification
```bash
ntm --robot-ensemble-synthesize=SESSION \
  --strategy=manual|adversarial|consensus|creative|analytical|deliberative|prioritized|dialectical|meta-reasoning|voting|argumentation-graph \
  --format=markdown|json|yaml \
  --output=/path/to/report.md \
  --force
```

## Behavior
1. Validate ensemble session exists and outputs collected
2. If outputs incomplete and `--force` not set, return error
3. Run OutputCollector + Synthesizer + DisagreementAuditor
4. Emit deterministic formatted output (markdown/json/yaml)
5. Return audit summary + output path

## Response Schema
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "timestamp": "2026-01-23T10:30:00Z",
  "action": "ensemble_synthesize",
  "session": "arch-review-2024-01-15",
  "status": "complete",
  "strategy": "adversarial",
  "report": {
    "summary": "...",
    "output_path": "/tmp/ensemble_report.md",
    "findings_count": 12,
    "recommendations_count": 7
  },
  "audit": {
    "conflict_count": 5,
    "unresolved": 2,
    "high_conflict_pairs": ["A1↔E2"]
  }
}
```

## Error Codes
- ENSEMBLE_NOT_FOUND
- SYNTHESIS_NOT_READY (outputs incomplete)
- OUTPUT_SCHEMA_INVALID
- INTERNAL_ERROR

## Logging Requirements
- Log start/end time and strategy
- Log number of outputs collected + invalid outputs
- Log audit conflict counts

## File Location
internal/robot/ensemble_synthesize.go

## Dependencies
- OutputCollector (bd-1av7i)
- Synthesis system (bd-2qwm8)
- DisagreementAuditor (bd-38go8)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:41:04.554611187Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:58:19.853721727Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:58:19.853638883Z","close_reason":"Implemented --robot-ensemble-synthesize command with strategy, format, output, and force options","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-rcwdt","depends_on_id":"bd-1av7i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-rcwdt","depends_on_id":"bd-2qwm8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-rcwdt","depends_on_id":"bd-38go8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,383 @@
+//go:build e2e
+// +build e2e
+
+// Package e2e contains end-to-end tests for NTM CLI commands.
+// [E2E-WEBHOOK] Tests for webhook delivery, retry, and signature handling.
+package e2e
+
+import (
+	"context"
+	"crypto/hmac"
+	"crypto/sha256"
+	"encoding/hex"
+	"encoding/json"
+	"fmt"
+	"io"
+	"net/http"
+	"net/http/httptest"
+	"os"
+	"os/exec"
+	"path/filepath"
+	"strings"
+	"sync/atomic"
+	"testing"
+	"time"
+)
+
+type WebhookPayload struct {
+	ID        string            `json:"id"`
+	Type      string            `json:"type"`
+	Timestamp time.Time         `json:"timestamp"`
+	Session   string            `json:"session,omitempty"`
+	Pane      string            `json:"pane,omitempty"`
+	Agent     string            `json:"agent,omitempty"`
+	Message   string            `json:"message"`
+	Details   map[string]string `json:"details,omitempty"`
+}
+
+type webhookCapture struct {
+	payload       WebhookPayload
+	raw           []byte
+	signature     string
+	eventTypeHdr  string
+	attemptHeader string
+}
+
+type WebhookTestSuite struct {
+	t          *testing.T
+	logger     *TestLogger
+	ntmPath    string
+	baseDir    string
+	projectDir string
+	session    string
+	cleanup    []func()
+}
+
+func NewWebhookTestSuite(t *testing.T, scenario string) *WebhookTestSuite {
+	logger := NewTestLogger(t, scenario)
+
+	ntmPath, err := exec.LookPath("ntm")
+	if err != nil {
+		t.Skip("ntm binary not found in PATH")
+	}
+
+	baseDir, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "ntm-webhook-e2e-")
+	if err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-WEBHOOK] Failed to create temp dir: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	session := fmt.Sprintf("e2e_webhook_%d", time.Now().UnixNano())
+	projectDir := filepath.Join(baseDir, session)
+	if err := os.MkdirAll(projectDir, 0755); err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-WEBHOOK] Failed to create project dir: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	suite := &WebhookTestSuite{
+		t:          t,
+		logger:     logger,
+		ntmPath:    ntmPath,
+		baseDir:    baseDir,
+		projectDir: projectDir,
+		session:    session,
+	}
+
+	suite.cleanup = append(suite.cleanup, func() {
+		os.RemoveAll(baseDir)
+	})
+
+	return suite
+}
+
+func (s *WebhookTestSuite) Cleanup() {
+	s.logger.Close()
+	for _, fn := range s.cleanup {
+		fn()
+	}
+}
+
+func (s *WebhookTestSuite) WriteConfig(contents string) {
+	path := filepath.Join(s.projectDir, ".ntm.yaml")
+	if err := os.WriteFile(path, []byte(contents), 0644); err != nil {
+		s.t.Fatalf("[E2E-WEBHOOK] write .ntm.yaml failed: %v", err)
+	}
+}
+
+func (s *WebhookTestSuite) env() []string {
+	return append(os.Environ(), "NTM_PROJECTS_BASE="+s.baseDir)
+}
+
+func (s *WebhookTestSuite) runNTM(ctx context.Context, args ...string) ([]byte, error) {
+	cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, s.ntmPath, args...)
+	cmd.Env = s.env()
+	cmd.Dir = s.projectDir
+	return cmd.CombinedOutput()
+}
+
+func (s *WebhookTestSuite) spawn(agentType string) error {
+	flag, ok := agentFlag(agentType)
+	if !ok {
+		return fmt.Errorf("unsupported agent type: %s", agentType)
+	}
+
+	args := []string{"--json", "spawn", s.session, "--no-hooks", flag}
+	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 2*time.Minute)
+	defer cancel()
+
+	s.logger.Log("[E2E-WEBHOOK] Running: ntm %s", strings.Join(args, " "))
+	out, err := s.runNTM(ctx, args...)
+	if err != nil {
+		s.logger.Log("[E2E-WEBHOOK] spawn error: %v output=%s", err, string(out))
+		return fmt.Errorf("spawn failed: %w output=%s", err, string(out))
+	}
+
+	return nil
+}
+
+func (s *WebhookTestSuite) kill() error {
+	args := []string{"--json", "kill", s.session, "--force"}
+	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 2*time.Minute)
+	defer cancel()
+
+	s.logger.Log("[E2E-WEBHOOK] Running: ntm %s", strings.Join(args, " "))
+	out, err := s.runNTM(ctx, args...)
+	if err != nil {
+		s.logger.Log("[E2E-WEBHOOK] kill error: %v output=%s", err, string(out))
+		return fmt.Errorf("kill failed: %w output=%s", err, string(out))
+	}
+	return nil
+}
+
+func agentFlag(agentType string) (string, bool) {
+	switch agentType {
+	case "cc", "claude":
+		return "--cc=1", true
+	case "cod", "codex":
+		return "--cod=1", true
+	case "gmi", "gemini":
+		return "--gmi=1", true
+	default:
+		return "", false
+	}
+}
+
+func waitForWebhook(t *testing.T, ch <-chan webhookCapture, timeout time.Duration) webhookCapture {
+	t.Helper()
+	select {
+	case capture := <-ch:
+		return capture
+	case <-time.After(timeout):
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-WEBHOOK] timeout waiting for webhook event")
+	}
+	return webhookCapture{}
+}
+
+func verifySignature(t *testing.T, secret string, capture webhookCapture) {
+	t.Helper()
+	if secret == "" {
+		return
+	}
+	if capture.signature == "" {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-WEBHOOK] missing X-NTM-Signature header")
+	}
+	mac := hmac.New(sha256.New, []byte(secret))
+	_, _ = mac.Write(capture.raw)
+	expected := "sha256=" + hex.EncodeToString(mac.Sum(nil))
+	if capture.signature != expected {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-WEBHOOK] signature mismatch: got %q want %q", capture.signature, expected)
+	}
+}
+
+// =============================================================================
+// Tests
+// =============================================================================
+
+func TestWebhookE2E_SessionCreated(t *testing.T) {
+	CommonE2EPrerequisites(t)
+	SkipIfNoAgents(t)
+
+	agentType := GetAvailableAgent()
+	if agentType == "" {
+		t.Skip("no agent CLI available")
+	}
+
+	suite := NewWebhookTestSuite(t, "webhook_session_created")
+	defer suite.Cleanup()
+
+	secret := "e2e-webhook-secret"
+	recv := make(chan webhookCapture, 1)
+
+	srv := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
+		defer r.Body.Close()
+		body, _ := io.ReadAll(r.Body)
+		var payload WebhookPayload
+		_ = json.Unmarshal(body, &payload)
+		recv <- webhookCapture{
+			payload:       payload,
+			raw:           body,
+			signature:     r.Header.Get("X-NTM-Signature"),
+			eventTypeHdr:  r.Header.Get("X-NTM-Event-Type"),
+			attemptHeader: r.Header.Get("X-NTM-Attempt"),
+		}
+		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
+	}))
+	defer srv.Close()
+
+	suite.WriteConfig(fmt.Sprintf(`
+webhooks:
+  - name: session_created
+    url: %s
+    events: ["session.created"]
+    formatter: json
+    secret: "%s"
+`, srv.URL, secret))
+
+	if err := suite.spawn(agentType); err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-WEBHOOK] spawn failed: %v", err)
+	}
+	defer suite.kill()
+
+	capture := waitForWebhook(t, recv, 30*time.Second)
+	suite.logger.LogJSON("[E2E-WEBHOOK] payload", capture.payload)
+
+	if capture.payload.Type != "session.created" {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-WEBHOOK] unexpected type: %q", capture.payload.Type)
+	}
+	if capture.payload.Session != suite.session {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-WEBHOOK] unexpected session: %q", capture.payload.Session)
+	}
+	if capture.eventTypeHdr != "session.created" {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-WEBHOOK] unexpected X-NTM-Event-Type: %q", capture.eventTypeHdr)
+	}
+
+	verifySignature(t, secret, capture)
+}
+
+func TestWebhookE2E_RetryOnFailure(t *testing.T) {
+	CommonE2EPrerequisites(t)
+	SkipIfNoAgents(t)
+
+	agentType := GetAvailableAgent()
+	if agentType == "" {
+		t.Skip("no agent CLI available")
+	}
+
+	suite := NewWebhookTestSuite(t, "webhook_retry")
+	defer suite.Cleanup()
+
+	var attempts int32
+	recv := make(chan webhookCapture, 1)
+
+	srv := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
+		defer r.Body.Close()
+		body, _ := io.ReadAll(r.Body)
+		var payload WebhookPayload
+		_ = json.Unmarshal(body, &payload)
+
+		n := atomic.AddInt32(&attempts, 1)
+		if n < 3 {
+			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusServiceUnavailable)
+			return
+		}
+		recv <- webhookCapture{
+			payload:       payload,
+			raw:           body,
+			signature:     r.Header.Get("X-NTM-Signature"),
+			eventTypeHdr:  r.Header.Get("X-NTM-Event-Type"),
+			attemptHeader: r.Header.Get("X-NTM-Attempt"),
+		}
+		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
+	}))
+	defer srv.Close()
+
+	suite.WriteConfig(fmt.Sprintf(`
+webhooks:
+  - name: session_killed
+    url: %s
+    events: ["session.killed"]
+    formatter: json
+    retry:
+      max_attempts: 3
+`, srv.URL))
+
+	if err := suite.spawn(agentType); err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-WEBHOOK] spawn failed: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	if err := suite.kill(); err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-WEBHOOK] kill failed: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	capture := waitForWebhook(t, recv, 30*time.Second)
+	suite.logger.LogJSON("[E2E-WEBHOOK] payload", capture.payload)
+
+	if capture.payload.Type != "session.killed" {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-WEBHOOK] unexpected type: %q", capture.payload.Type)
+	}
+
+	if got := atomic.LoadInt32(&attempts); got < 3 {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-WEBHOOK] expected retries (>=3 attempts), got %d", got)
+	}
+}
+
+func TestWebhookE2E_MultipleEndpoints(t *testing.T) {
+	CommonE2EPrerequisites(t)
+	SkipIfNoAgents(t)
+
+	agentType := GetAvailableAgent()
+	if agentType == "" {
+		t.Skip("no agent CLI available")
+	}
+
+	suite := NewWebhookTestSuite(t, "webhook_multiple_endpoints")
+	defer suite.Cleanup()
+
+	recvA := make(chan webhookCapture, 1)
+	recvB := make(chan webhookCapture, 1)
+
+	srvA := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
+		defer r.Body.Close()
+		body, _ := io.ReadAll(r.Body)
+		var payload WebhookPayload
+		_ = json.Unmarshal(body, &payload)
+		recvA <- webhookCapture{payload: payload}
+		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
+	}))
+	defer srvA.Close()
+
+	srvB := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
+		defer r.Body.Close()
+		body, _ := io.ReadAll(r.Body)
+		var payload WebhookPayload
+		_ = json.Unmarshal(body, &payload)
+		recvB <- webhookCapture{payload: payload}
+		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
+	}))
+	defer srvB.Close()
+
+	suite.WriteConfig(fmt.Sprintf(`
+webhooks:
+  - name: endpoint_a
+    url: %s
+    events: ["session.created"]
+    formatter: json
+  - name: endpoint_b
+    url: %s
+    events: ["session.created"]
+    formatter: json
+`, srvA.URL, srvB.URL))
+
+	if err := suite.spawn(agentType); err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-WEBHOOK] spawn failed: %v", err)
+	}
+	defer suite.kill()
+
+	captureA := waitForWebhook(t, recvA, 30*time.Second)
+	captureB := waitForWebhook(t, recvB, 30*time.Second)
+
+	if captureA.payload.Type != "session.created" || captureB.payload.Type != "session.created" {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-WEBHOOK] expected session.created for both endpoints")
+	}
+	if captureA.payload.Session != suite.session || captureB.payload.Session != suite.session {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-WEBHOOK] expected session %q for both endpoints", suite.session)
+	}
+}
